1899.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abu Musa al-Ash'ari sought permission to enter upon 'Umar three times but was not given permission, so he turned back. He said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'When one who seeks permission has asked three times, if he is permitted to enter then he may enter, otherwise he should leave.'
1899.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abu Musa al-Asy'ari meminta izin kepada Umar tiga kali namun tidak diizinkan, lalu ia pun pergi. Ia berkata: Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila seseorang yang meminta izin telah meminta tiga kali, jika ia diizinkan maka ia boleh masuk, jika tidak maka hendaklah ia pergi.'

1900.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn 'Abdillah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: I came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and knocked on his door. He said: 'Who is there?' I said: 'Me.' He said: 'Me? Me?!' and he disliked that.
1900.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn 'Abdillah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Aku datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan mengetuk pintunya. Beliau berkata: 'Siapa itu?' Aku berkata: 'Aku.' Beliau berkata: 'Aku? Aku?!' dan beliau pun tidak menyukainya.

1901.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn 'Abdillah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade a man from coming to his family suddenly at night, or accusing them of treachery, or seeking out their faults.
1901.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn 'Abdillah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melarang seorang laki-laki mendatangi keluarganya secara tiba-tiba di malam hari, atau menuduh mereka berkhianat, atau mencari-cari kesalahan mereka.

1902.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdallah ibn Salam (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ arrived in Madinah, the people went out to see him. He said: 'O people, spread the salam, feed others, maintain family ties, and pray at night while people are sleeping — you will enter Paradise in peace.'
1902.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdallah ibn Salam (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ tiba di Madinah, beliau bersabda: 'Wahai manusia, sebarkanlah salam, berilah makan, sambunglah silaturrahim, dan shalatlah di malam hari saat manusia sedang tidur — niscaya kalian akan masuk surga dengan selamat.'

1903.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A Muslim has six duties toward his fellow Muslim: greet him when he meets him; say yarhamukallah when he sneezes; visit him when he is sick; respond when he invites him; attend his funeral; and love for him what he loves for himself, and be sincere to him in his absence.'
1903.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang Muslim memiliki enam kewajiban terhadap sesama Muslim: mengucapkan salam ketika bertemu; mendoakannya ketika bersin; menjenguknya ketika sakit; memenuhi undangannya; menghadiri jenazahnya; dan mencintai untuknya apa yang ia cintai untuk dirinya sendiri, serta menasihatinya ketika ia tidak ada.'

1904.iwh_en: It was narrated from Fadalah ibn 'Ubayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The rider greets the one on foot; the one standing greets the one sitting; and the smaller group greets the larger group.'
1904.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Fadalah ibn 'Ubayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Yang berkendaraan memberi salam kepada yang berjalan kaki; yang berdiri memberi salam kepada yang duduk; dan yang sedikit memberi salam kepada yang banyak.'

1905.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of the Jews greets you, he says: al-sam 'alayk (death be upon you). So say: wa 'alayk (and upon you).'
1905.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang dari orang-orang Yahudi memberi salam, maka sesungguhnya ia mengatakan: al-sam 'alayk (kematian atasmu). Maka ucapkanlah: wa 'alayk (dan atasmu pula).'

1906.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ passed by some children and greeted them with salam.
1906.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ melewati sekelompok anak-anak lalu mengucapkan salam kepada mereka.

1907.iwh_en: It was narrated from Asma' bint Yazid ibn al-Sakan (may Allah be pleased with her) that while she was among a group of women, the Prophet ﷺ passed by them and greeted them with salam.
1907.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Asma' binti Yazid ibn al-Sakan (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ketika ia sedang bersama sekelompok wanita, Nabi ﷺ melewati mereka lalu mengucapkan salam kepada mereka.

1908.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'A'ishah (may Allah be pleased with her), wife of the Prophet ﷺ, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O 'A'ishah, this is Jibril sending you his salam.' She said: 'Wa 'alayhi al-salam wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh.' She said: And he sees what I cannot see.
1908.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha), istri Nabi ﷺ, bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Wahai 'Aisyah, ini Jibril menyampaikan salam kepadamu.' Ia berkata: 'Wa 'alayhi al-salam wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh.' Ia berkata: Dan ia (Nabi) melihat apa yang aku tidak bisa melihatnya.

1909.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) that after the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had finished his prayer, he approached him and was the first to greet him with the greeting of Islam. The Prophet said: 'Wa 'alayk al-salam wa rahmatullah — from which tribe are you?' He said: 'From Ghifar.' He then moved his hand, and Abu Dharr said to himself: It seems he disliked that I am from Ghifar.
1909.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dzarr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa setelah Rasulullah ﷺ selesai shalat, ia mendatanginya dan menjadi orang pertama yang memberi salam dengan salam Islam. Nabi bersabda: 'Wa 'alayk al-salam wa rahmatullah — dari suku mana engkau?' Ia berkata: 'Dari Ghifar.' Lalu Nabi menggerakkan tangannya, dan Abu Dzarr berkata dalam hatinya: Sepertinya beliau tidak suka bahwa aku berasal dari Ghifar.

1910.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Imran ibn Husayn (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'Al-salamu 'alaykum.' He responded and said: 'Ten.' Then another man came and said: 'Al-salamu 'alaykum wa rahmatullah.' He responded and said: 'Twenty.' Then another man came and said: 'Al-salamu 'alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh.' He responded and said: 'Thirty.'
1910.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Imran ibn Hushayn (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Al-salamu 'alaykum.' Beliau membalasnya dan berkata: 'Sepuluh.' Kemudian datang laki-laki lain dan berkata: 'Al-salamu 'alaykum wa rahmatullah.' Beliau membalasnya dan berkata: 'Dua puluh.' Kemudian datang laki-laki lain dan berkata: 'Al-salamu 'alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh.' Beliau membalasnya dan berkata: 'Tiga puluh.'

1911.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Muhajir ibn Qunfudh (may Allah be pleased with him) that he greeted the Prophet ﷺ while he was urinating, and the Prophet did not return his salam until he had performed wudu, and then he returned it.
1911.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Muhajir ibn Qunfudh (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mengucapkan salam kepada Nabi ﷺ saat beliau sedang buang air kecil, maka Nabi tidak membalas salamnya hingga beliau berwudhu, kemudian barulah beliau membalasnya.

1912.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Uqbah ibn 'Amir (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not enter upon women.' It was said: O Messenger of Allah, what about the husband's male relatives? He said: 'The husband's male relative is death.'
1912.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Uqbah ibn 'Amir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian masuk menemui para wanita.' Dikatakan: Wahai Rasulullah, bagaimana dengan kerabat suami? Beliau bersabda: 'Kerabat suami adalah kematian.'

1913.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jarir ibn 'Abdillah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he asked the Prophet ﷺ about an accidental glance, and the Prophet said: 'Turn your gaze away.'
1913.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jarir ibn 'Abdillah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ tentang pandangan yang terjadi secara tiba-tiba (tidak disengaja), maka Nabi bersabda: 'Palingkan pandanganmu.'

1914.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her), wife of the Prophet ﷺ, that the Prophet was asked about the hem of a woman's garment. He said: 'A hand-span.' She said: O Messenger of Allah, then her feet will show. He said: 'Then a forearm-length — let her not exceed that.'
1914.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anha), istri Nabi ﷺ, bahwa Nabi ditanya tentang ujung pakaian wanita. Beliau bersabda: 'Sejengkal.' Ia berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, kalau begitu telapak kaki mereka akan terlihat. Beliau bersabda: 'Maka sehasta — jangan melebihi itu.'

1915.iwh_en: It was narrated from a sister of Hudhayfah (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ addressed the women and said: 'O women, is not silver enough for you to adorn yourselves with? Indeed, no woman among you adorns herself with gold and displays it except that she will be punished by it.'
1915.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari saudara perempuan Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah kepada para wanita dan bersabda: 'Wahai kaum wanita, tidakkah cukup bagi kalian untuk berhias dengan perak? Sesungguhnya tidaklah seorang wanita di antara kalian yang berhias dengan emas lalu menampakkannya, kecuali ia akan disiksa dengannya.'

1916.iwh_en: Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Any woman who wears perfume and then goes out so that her scent may be noticed is a fornicatress, and every eye that looks is guilty of fornication.'
1916.iwh_id: Abu Musa al-Asy'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Wanita mana saja yang memakai parfum kemudian keluar agar wanginya dapat dicium orang lain, maka ia adalah pezina, dan setiap mata yang memandangnya adalah berzina.'

1917.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdallah (ibn Mas'ud) (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Allah has cursed the tattooers and the tattooed, those who pluck eyebrows, and those who file their teeth for beauty — those who alter Allah's creation.' This reached a woman from Banu Asad known as Umm Ya'qub, and she came and said: I have heard that you cursed such-and-such. He said: Why should I not curse those whom the Messenger of Allah ﷺ cursed, and it is in the Book of Allah?
1917.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdallah (ibn Mas'ud) (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Allah telah melaknat para wanita yang membuat tato dan yang ditato, yang mencabut alis, dan yang mengikir gigi demi kecantikan — mereka yang mengubah ciptaan Allah.' Hal ini sampai kepada seorang wanita dari Bani Asad yang disebut Umm Ya'qub, lalu ia datang dan berkata: Aku mendengar bahwa engkau melaknat ini dan itu. Ia berkata: Mengapa aku tidak melaknat orang-orang yang dilaknat Rasulullah ﷺ, dan hal itu ada dalam Kitab Allah?

1918.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Rayhanah (may Allah be pleased with him), a companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to forbid ten things: a man lying with another man under a single garment with nothing between them; a woman lying with another woman under a single garment with nothing between them; plucking; tattooing; looting; riding on the backs of leopards; and wearing silk on the shoulders or at the hem of garments.
1918.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Rayhanah (radhiyallahu 'anhu), sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ, bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa melarang sepuluh hal: seorang laki-laki berbaring bersama laki-laki lain dalam satu pakaian tanpa pembatas; seorang wanita berbaring bersama wanita lain dalam satu pakaian tanpa pembatas; mencabut bulu; membuat tato; merampas harta; menunggang macan tutul; dan memakai sutera di bagian bahu atau di bagian bawah pakaian.

1919.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ cursed men who imitate women and women who imitate men, and said: 'Expel them from your homes.'
1919.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Nabi ﷺ melaknat laki-laki yang menyerupai perempuan dan perempuan yang menyerupai laki-laki, dan bersabda: 'Keluarkan mereka dari rumah-rumah kalian.'

1920.iwh_en: It was narrated that he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sat with us while my thigh was uncovered. He said: 'Cover yourself — don't you know that the thigh is 'awrah?'
1920.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa ia berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ duduk bersama kami sementara pahaku terbuka. Beliau bersabda: 'Tutupilah dirimu — tidakkah kamu tahu bahwa paha adalah aurat?'

1921.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'A'ishah (may Allah be pleased with her) that some women from Hims came to her seeking a ruling, and she said: 'Perhaps you are from those women who enter public baths?' They said: Yes. She said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'No woman who removes her clothing anywhere other than her husband's home except that she tears away the veil between herself and Allah.'
1921.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa beberapa wanita dari Hims datang kepadanya meminta fatwa, lalu ia berkata: 'Mungkin kalian termasuk wanita-wanita yang masuk ke tempat pemandian umum?' Mereka berkata: Ya. Ia berkata: Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidaklah seorang wanita yang menanggalkan pakaiannya di tempat selain rumah suaminya, kecuali ia telah merobek tabir antara dirinya dan Allah.'

1922.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A man should not make his brother rise from his seat and then sit in it himself, but rather make room and give space.'
1922.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang laki-laki tidak boleh meminta saudaranya berdiri dari tempat duduknya lalu ia duduk di sana, tetapi hendaklah kalian melapangkan dan memberi ruang.'

1923.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you gets up from his seat and then returns to it, he has more right to it.'
1923.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang di antara kalian berdiri dari tempat duduknya kemudian kembali ke sana, maka ia lebih berhak atas tempat duduk tersebut.'

1924.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Bara' ibn 'Azib (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ passed by some Ansari men sitting, and said: 'If you must sit, then guide the way, spread the salam, and help the oppressed.'
1924.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Bara' ibn 'Azib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melewati sekelompok orang Anshar yang sedang duduk dan bersabda: 'Jika kalian harus duduk, maka tunjukkanlah jalan, sebarkanlah salam, dan bantulah orang yang dizalimi.'

1925.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abbad ibn Tamim's uncle (may Allah be pleased with him) that he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ lying on his back in the mosque with one leg resting on the other.
1925.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari paman 'Abbad ibn Tamim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ berbaring terlentang di masjid dengan satu kakinya diletakkan di atas yang lain.

1926.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdallah (ibn Mas'ud) (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When you are three, two of you should not converse privately excluding the third, for that will grieve him.'
1926.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdallah (ibn Mas'ud) (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila kalian bertiga, maka dua orang tidak boleh berbisik-bisik mengecualikan yang ketiga, karena hal itu akan membuatnya sedih.'

1927.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Barzah al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) that in his later years, when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sat in a gathering and wanted to rise, he would say: 'Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, ashhadu an la ilaha illa anta, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilayk.' They asked: O Messenger of Allah, you now say words you did not say before. He said: 'This is an expiation for what takes place in gatherings.'
1927.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Barzah al-Aslami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa di akhir hayatnya, apabila Rasulullah ﷺ duduk dalam suatu majelis dan hendak berdiri, beliau membaca: 'Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, asyhadu an la ilaha illa anta, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilayk.' Mereka bertanya: Wahai Rasulullah, engkau kini mengucapkan kalimat yang dulu tidak pernah engkau ucapkan. Beliau bersabda: 'Ini adalah kafarat bagi apa yang terjadi dalam majelis.'

1928.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The one who sneezes says: al-hamdulillah 'ala kulli hal. And the one who responds says: yarhamukumullah. And he replies: yahdikumullahu wa yuslih balakum.'
1928.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang yang bersin mengucapkan: al-hamdulillahi 'ala kulli hal. Yang merespons mengucapkan: yarhamukumullah. Dan ia membalas: yahdikumullahu wa yushlih balakum.'

1929.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that two men sneezed in the presence of the Prophet ﷺ and he said yarhamuk to one of them but not to the other. He was asked: O Messenger of Allah, you blessed this one but not the other. He said: 'This one praised Allah, and that one did not praise Allah.'
1929.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dua orang bersin di hadapan Nabi ﷺ, lalu beliau mendoakan rahmat bagi salah satunya namun tidak bagi yang lain. Ditanyakan kepada beliau: Wahai Rasulullah, engkau mendoakan yang ini tapi tidak yang itu. Beliau bersabda: 'Yang ini memuji Allah, sedangkan yang itu tidak memuji Allah.'

1930.iwh_en: It was narrated from Salamah ibn al-Akwa' (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man sneezed in the presence of the Prophet ﷺ and the Prophet said: 'Yarhamukallah.' Then he sneezed again, and the Prophet said: 'The man has a cold.'
1930.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Salamah ibn al-Akwa' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bersin di hadapan Nabi ﷺ, maka Nabi bersabda: 'Yarhamukallah.' Kemudian ia bersin lagi, maka Nabi bersabda: 'Orang ini sedang pilek.'

1931.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'A'ishah (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: We had a cloth with images on it. I placed it in front of the Prophet ﷺ while he was praying, and he forbade me — or she said: he disapproved. She said: So I made it into cushions.
1931.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: Kami memiliki sehelai kain yang bergambar. Aku meletakkannya di depan Nabi ﷺ saat beliau sedang shalat, maka beliau melarangku — atau ia berkata: beliau tidak menyukainya. Ia berkata: Maka aku menjadikannya sebagai bantal-bantal.

1932.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The angels do not enter a house in which there is a dog, an image, or someone in a state of major ritual impurity.'
1932.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya malaikat tidak masuk ke dalam rumah yang di dalamnya terdapat anjing, gambar (patung/lukisan makhluk bernyawa), atau orang yang dalam keadaan junub.'

1933.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Mas'ud al-Badri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'When a Muslim spends on his family seeking reward from Allah, it is sadaqah for him.'
1933.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Mas'ud al-Badri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila seorang Muslim menafkahi keluarganya dengan mengharap pahala dari Allah, maka itu adalah sedekah baginya.'

1934.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdallah ibn Ja'far (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ returned from a journey, he would be welcomed by him and al-Hasan or al-Husayn. He said: He carried me in front of him and al-Hasan behind him until we entered Madinah on the same mount as the Prophet ﷺ.
1934.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdallah ibn Ja'far (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa apabila Rasulullah ﷺ kembali dari perjalanan, beliau disambut olehnya dan al-Hasan atau al-Husain. Ia berkata: Beliau membawaku di depannya dan al-Hasan di belakangnya hingga kami memasuki Madinah di atas kendaraan yang sama dengan Nabi ﷺ.

1935.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdallah ibn Hanzalah ibn al-Ghasil (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A man has the most right to the front of his own mount, the front of his own bed, and to lead the prayer in his own home.'
1935.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdallah ibn Hanzalah ibn al-Ghasil (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang laki-laki lebih berhak atas bagian depan kendaraannya, bagian depan tempat tidurnya, dan untuk menjadi imam shalat di rumahnya.'

1936.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hamzah ibn 'Amr al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'On the hump of every camel there is a shaytan, so when you ride them, say the name of Allah, and do not fall short of your needs.'
1936.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hamzah ibn 'Amr al-Aslami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Di atas punuk setiap unta terdapat setan, maka apabila kalian menungganginya ucapkanlah nama Allah, dan janganlah kalian melewatkan kebutuhan kalian.'

1937.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Anas (may Allah be pleased with him), one of the companions of the Prophet ﷺ, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Ride these animals in good health, and do not use them as chairs.'
1937.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adh ibn Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu), salah seorang sahabat Nabi ﷺ, bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tunggangilah hewan-hewan ini dengan sehat (tidak berlebihan), dan janganlah kalian menjadikannya sebagai kursi-kursi.'

1938.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Travel is a piece of punishment — it prevents one of you from his sleep, food, and drink. So when one of you has accomplished his purpose, let him hasten back to his family.'
1938.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Perjalanan adalah sepotong siksa — ia menghalangi salah seorang di antara kalian dari tidur, makan, dan minumnya. Maka apabila salah seorang di antara kalian telah menyelesaikan keperluannya, hendaklah ia segera kembali kepada keluarganya.'

1939.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: O Prophet of Allah, I intend to travel. He said: 'When?' He said: Tomorrow, if Allah wills. He came and the Prophet took his hand and said: 'May Allah guard you and protect you. May Allah grant you taqwa, forgive your sins, and direct you to good wherever you turn.'
1939.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: Wahai Nabi Allah, aku ingin bepergian. Beliau berkata: 'Kapan?' Ia berkata: Besok, insya Allah. Ia pun datang dan Nabi memegang tangannya lalu bersabda: 'Semoga Allah menjagamu dan melindungimu. Semoga Allah menganugerahimu ketakwaan, mengampuni dosamu, dan mengarahkanmu kepada kebaikan ke mana pun engkau menuju.'

1940.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdallah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Prophet ﷺ traveled, he would say: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardships of travel, from returning to grief, from loss after gain, from the supplication of the oppressed, and from an evil sight regarding my family and wealth.'
1940.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdallah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa apabila Nabi ﷺ bepergian, beliau membaca: 'Allahumma inni a'udzubika min wa'tha'i al-safar wa ka'abati al-munqalab wa al-hawr ba'da al-kawr wa da'wati al-mazhlum wa su'i al-manzhar fi al-ahli wa al-mal (Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kesulitan perjalanan, dari kesedihan saat kembali, dari kemunduran setelah kemajuan, dari doa orang yang dizalimi, dan dari pemandangan buruk pada keluarga dan harta).'

1941.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ traveled and mounted his riding beast, he would say Allahu Akbar three times and then recite: 'Glory be to the One Who has subjected this to us, for we could not have done so ourselves, and indeed to our Lord we shall return. O Allah, I ask You in this journey for righteousness and piety, and for deeds that please You. O Allah, ease our journey and fold for us the distance of the earth. O Allah, You are the Companion in travel and the Successor over my family. O Allah, accompany us in our journey and be the Guardian of our family in goodness.'
1941.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa apabila Rasulullah ﷺ bepergian dan menaiki kendaraannya, beliau bertakbir tiga kali lalu membaca: 'Subhana alladzi sakhkhara lana hadza wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin wa inna ila rabbina la munqalibun. Allahumma inna nas'aluka fi safarina hadza al-birra wa al-taqwa wa mina al-'amali ma tardha. Allahumma hawwin 'alayna al-safar wa athwi lana bu'da al-ardh. Allahumma anta al-sahib fi al-safar wa al-khalifah fi al-ahl. Allahumma ish-habna fi safarina wa ukhlufna fi ahlina bi khayr.'

1942.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: When we ascended we would say Allahu Akbar, and when we descended we would say Subhanallah.
1942.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Apabila kami mendaki kami mengucapkan Allahu Akbar, dan apabila kami turun kami mengucapkan Subhanallah.

1943.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm Habibah (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Angels do not accompany a caravan in which there are bells.'
1943.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm Habibah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Malaikat tidak menemani rombongan yang di dalamnya terdapat lonceng.'

1944.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Angels do not accompany a traveling party in which there is a dog or a bell.'
1944.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Malaikat tidak menemani rombongan perjalanan yang di dalamnya terdapat anjing atau lonceng.'

1945.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Imran ibn Husayn (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ was on a journey when he heard a curse, and asked: 'What is this?' They said: A woman has cursed her mount. He said: 'Take the load off it, for it is accursed.' So they removed it.
1945.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Imran ibn Hushayn (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ sedang dalam perjalanan ketika mendengar seseorang mengutuk, lalu beliau bertanya: 'Apa ini?' Mereka berkata: Seorang wanita mengutuk kendaraannya. Beliau bersabda: 'Lepaskanlah muatannya karena ia telah dilaknat.' Maka mereka pun melepaskannya.

1946.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A woman may not travel for three days or more except with her father, brother, husband, or another mahram.'
1946.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang wanita tidak boleh bepergian selama tiga hari atau lebih kecuali bersama ayahnya, saudaranya, suaminya, atau mahram lainnya.'

1947.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'If people knew what lies in traveling alone, no rider would ever travel alone at night.'
1947.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Seandainya manusia mengetahui apa yang terdapat dalam kesendirian, niscaya tidak ada seorang pengendara pun yang mau menempuh perjalanan sendirian di malam hari.'

1948.iwh_en: It was narrated from Khawlah bint Hakim (may Allah be pleased with her) that she heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'If one of you stops at a place and says: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created — nothing will harm him in that place until he departs.'
1948.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Khaulah binti Hakim (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa di antara kalian singgah di suatu tempat dan mengucapkan: A'udzubikalimatillahi al-tammati min syarri ma khalaq — maka tidak ada sesuatu pun yang akan membahayakannya di tempat itu hingga ia pergi.'

1949.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ, when he stopped at a place, would not depart until he had prayed two rak'ahs bidding farewell to that place.
1949.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ apabila singgah di suatu tempat, tidak akan meninggalkannya hingga beliau shalat dua rakaat untuk mengucapkan selamat tinggal kepada tempat itu.

1950.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ, when returning from a journey, would say: 'A'ibun, in sha'Allah, ta'ibun, 'abidun, li rabbina hamidun (We are returning, if Allah wills — repenting, worshipping, to our Lord giving praise).'
1950.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Nabi ﷺ apabila kembali dari perjalanan membaca: 'A'ibuna insya'Allah, ta'ibuna, 'abiduna, li rabbina hamidun (Kami kembali, insya Allah — bertaubat, beribadah, memuji Tuhan kami).'

1951.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Bara' ibn 'Azib (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded a man that when he takes to his bed, he should say: 'O Allah, I have submitted myself to You, turned my face to You, entrusted my affairs to You, and leaned my back on You, in hope and fear of You. There is no refuge and no escape from You except to You. I believe in Your Book which You revealed and Your Prophet whom You sent. If he dies (having said this), he dies upon the fitrah.'
1951.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Bara' ibn 'Azib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan seseorang agar ketika ia berbaring hendaklah mengucapkan: 'Allahumma aslamtu nafsi ilayk, wa wajjahtu wajhi ilayk, wa fawwadhtu amri ilayk, wa alja'tu zhahri ilayk, raghbatan wa rahbatan ilayk, la malja'a wa la manja minka illa ilayk, amantu bikitabika alladzi anzalt wa nabiyyika alladzi arsalt. Jika ia meninggal (setelah mengucapkan ini), ia meninggal di atas fitrah.'

1952.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you goes to his bed, let him dust off his bed with the inside of his lower garment, for he does not know what has been left on it. Then let him say: O Allah, with You I lay down my side, and with You I raise it. O Allah, if You take my soul, then have mercy on it. And if You release it, then protect it as You protect Your righteous servants.'
1952.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang di antara kalian mendatangi tempat tidurnya, hendaklah ia membersihkan tempat tidurnya dengan bagian dalam sarungnya, karena ia tidak tahu apa yang ada di sana. Kemudian hendaklah ia mengucapkan: Allahumma bika wadha'tu janbi wa bika arfa'uhu. Allahumma in amsakta nafsi faghfir laha wa in arsaltaha fahfazha bima tahfazhu bihi 'ibadaka al-shalihin.'

1953.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to him and Fatimah and taught them what to say when going to bed: thirty-three tasbihahs, thirty-three tahmihahs, and thirty-four takbirahs. 'Ali said: I have not left this practice since. He was asked: Not even the night of Siffin? He said: Not even the night of Siffin.
1953.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ datang kepadanya dan Fatimah lalu mengajarkan mereka apa yang harus diucapkan ketika hendak tidur: tiga puluh tiga tasbih, tiga puluh tiga tahmid, dan tiga puluh empat takbir. 'Ali berkata: Aku tidak pernah meninggalkan amalan ini sejak saat itu. Ia ditanya: Bahkan malam Shiffin? Ia berkata: Bahkan malam Shiffin.

1954.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfah (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Prophet ﷺ woke up he would say: 'Al-hamdulillahi alladhi ahyana ba'da ma amatana wa ilayhi al-nushur (Praise be to Allah Who has given us life after causing us to die, and to Him is the resurrection).'
1954.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa apabila Nabi ﷺ terbangun, beliau membaca: 'Al-hamdulillahi alladzi ahyana ba'da ma amatana wa ilayhi al-nushur (Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menghidupkan kami setelah mematikan kami, dan kepada-Nya lah kebangkitan).'

1955.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Ubadah ibn al-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever wakes up at night and says: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lahu, lahu al-mulku wa lahu al-hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir, subhanallahi wa al-hamdulillahi wa allahu akbar wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah — then says: Lord, forgive me — or then makes du'a — it will be answered. And if he gets up, performs wudu, and prays, his prayer will be accepted.'
1955.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Ubadah ibn al-Shamit (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang terbangun di malam hari lalu mengucapkan: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la syarika lahu, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli syai'in qadir, subhanallahi wa al-hamdulillahi wa allahu akbar wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah — kemudian berkata: Rabbi ighfir li — atau kemudian berdoa — maka akan dikabulkan. Dan jika ia bangkit lalu berwudhu lalu shalat, maka shalatnya akan diterima.'

1956.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abza (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Prophet ﷺ entered the morning, he would say: 'We have entered the morning upon the fitrah of Islam, upon the word of sincerity, upon the religion of our Prophet Muhammad, and upon the way of our father Ibrahim — a hanif and a Muslim.'
1956.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abza (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa apabila Nabi ﷺ memasuki pagi hari, beliau mengucapkan: 'Ashbahna 'ala fitratil-Islam wa kalimatil-ikhlas wa din nabiyyina Muhammad wa millati abina Ibrahim hanifan musliman (Kami memasuki pagi hari di atas fitrah Islam, kalimat ikhlas, agama Nabi kami Muhammad, dan millah bapak kami Ibrahim — seorang yang hanif dan Muslim).'

1957.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abu Bakr said: O Messenger of Allah, command me with something to say in the morning and in the evening. He said: 'Say: O Allah, Creator of the heavens and earth, Knower of the unseen and the seen, Lord and Sovereign of all things — I bear witness that there is no god but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my soul and from the evil of the shaytan and his shirk.' He said: 'Say it in the morning and in the evening, and when you take to your bed.'
1957.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abu Bakr berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, perintahkan aku dengan sesuatu yang aku ucapkan di pagi dan di sore hari. Beliau bersabda: 'Ucapkan: Allahumma fathira al-samawati wa al-ardh, 'alima al-ghaybi wa al-shahadah, rabba kulli syai'in wa malikahu, asyhadu an la ilaha illa anta, a'udzubika min syarri nafsi wa min syarril-syaythani wa syirkihi.' Beliau berkata: 'Ucapkanlah di pagi hari, di sore hari, dan ketika hendak tidur.'

1958.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever puts on a garment and says: Al-hamdulillahi alladhi kasani hadha wa razaqanihi min ghayri hawlin minni wa la quwwah (Praise be to Allah Who clothed me with this and provided it for me without any strength or power from me) — his past sins will be forgiven.'
1958.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adh ibn Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa memakai pakaian lalu mengucapkan: Al-hamdulillahi alladzi kasani hadza wa razaqanihi min ghayri hawlin minni wa la quwwah (Segala puji bagi Allah yang mengenakan pakaian ini padaku dan memberikannya kepadaku tanpa daya dan kekuatan dariku) — maka dosa-dosanya yang telah lalu akan diampuni.'

1959.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Humayd or Abu Usayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you enters the mosque, let him say: O Allah, open for me the gates of Your mercy. And when he leaves, let him say: O Allah, I ask You of Your bounty.'
1959.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Humayd atau Abu Usayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang di antara kalian masuk ke masjid, hendaklah mengucapkan: Allahumma iftah li abwaba rahmatik (Ya Allah, bukakanlah untukku pintu-pintu rahmat-Mu). Dan apabila ia keluar, hendaklah mengucapkan: Allahumma inni as'aluka min fadhlika (Ya Allah, aku memohon kepada-Mu dari karunia-Mu).'

1960.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdallah ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever enters the marketplace and says: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lahu, lahu al-mulku wa lahu al-hamdu, yuhyi wa yumitu wa huwa hayyun la yamut, biyadihi al-khayr wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir — Allah will record for him a million good deeds, erase from him a million sins, and raise him a million degrees.'
1960.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdallah ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa masuk ke pasar lalu mengucapkan: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la syarika lahu, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu, yuhyi wa yumitu wa huwa hayyun la yamut, biyadihi al-khayr wa huwa 'ala kulli syai'in qadir — Allah akan mencatat untuknya sejuta kebaikan, menghapus darinya sejuta keburukan, dan mengangkatnya sejuta derajat.'

1961.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'You may use my name, but do not use my kunya (Abu al-Qasim).'
1961.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Berilah nama (seperti) namaku, namun janganlah menggunakan kunyahku (Abu al-Qasim).'

1962.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'You will be called on the Day of Resurrection by your names and your fathers' names, so give your children good names.'
1962.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya kalian akan dipanggil pada Hari Kiamat dengan nama-nama kalian dan nama-nama bapak kalian, maka perbaikilah nama-nama kalian.'

1963.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The most beloved names to Allah are: 'Abdallah and 'Abd al-Rahman.'
1963.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Nama-nama yang paling dicintai Allah adalah: 'Abdallah dan 'Abd al-Rahman.'

1964.iwh_en: It was narrated from Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ forbade naming our slaves four names: Aflah, Nafi', Rabah, and Yasar.
1964.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Samurah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ melarang kami memberi nama budak-budak kami dengan empat nama: Aflah, Nafi', Rabah, dan Yasar.

1965.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the mother of 'Asim was called 'Asiyah, and the Prophet ﷺ renamed her Jamilah.
1965.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ibu dari 'Ashim dipanggil 'Ashiyah, maka Nabi ﷺ mengganti namanya menjadi Jamilah.

1966.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that Zaynab's name used to be Barrah, and the Prophet ﷺ renamed her Zaynab.
1966.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa nama Zainab dahulunya adalah Barrah, maka Nabi ﷺ mengganti namanya menjadi Zainab.

1967.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Tufayl, the brother of 'A'ishah (may Allah be pleased with him), that a mushrik said to a Muslim: 'What a fine people you are, except that you say: Whatever Allah wills and Muhammad wills.' The Prophet ﷺ heard this and said: 'Do not say: Whatever Allah wills and Muhammad wills. Rather say: Whatever Allah wills, then what Muhammad wills.'
1967.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Thufayl, saudara 'Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu), bahwa seorang musyrik berkata kepada seorang Muslim: 'Alangkah baiknya kaum kalian, kecuali bahwa kalian berkata: Apa yang Allah kehendaki dan yang Muhammad kehendaki.' Nabi ﷺ mendengar ini lalu bersabda: 'Janganlah berkata: Apa yang Allah kehendaki dan yang Muhammad kehendaki. Melainkan katakanlah: Apa yang Allah kehendaki, kemudian apa yang Muhammad kehendaki.'

1968.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not call the grape vineyard al-karm (the noble one). Indeed, al-karm (the truly noble) is the Muslim man.'
1968.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah menyebut kebun anggur dengan al-karm (yang mulia). Sesungguhnya al-karm yang sejati adalah laki-laki Muslim.'

1969.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that there was a young man driving the Prophet's wives' camels, and the Prophet ﷺ said: 'O Anjasha, take it easy — you are driving crystal vessels.'
1969.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ada seorang pemuda yang menggiring unta-unta istri Nabi ﷺ, maka Nabi bersabda: 'Wahai Anjasyah, pelan-pelanlah dalam menggiring — kamu sedang menggiring bejana-bejana kristal.'

1970.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Haydah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Woe to the one who speaks and lies in order to make people laugh — woe to him, woe to him!'
1970.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah ibn Hayda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Celakalah orang yang berbicara dan berdusta untuk membuat orang-orang tertawa — celakalah dia! Celakalah dia!'

1971.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ confirmed two verses of Umayyah ibn Abi al-Salt's poetry, saying 'He spoke the truth' to each.
1971.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Nabi ﷺ membenarkan dua bait syair Umayyah ibn Abi al-Shalt, dan berkata 'Ia benar' untuk masing-masingnya.

1972.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubayy ibn Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Indeed some poetry contains wisdom.'
1972.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubayy ibn Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya sebagian dari syair mengandung hikmah.'

1973.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'For one of you to fill his belly with pus or blood is better than filling it with poetry.'
1973.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Bahwa perut salah seorang di antara kalian dipenuhi dengan nanah atau darah adalah lebih baik daripada dipenuhi dengan syair.'
